profile.name: Lisset Dayana
profile.professionalTitle: BSc in Radiochemistry | Computational Chemistry | Molecular Modeling | Bioactivity and Nuclear Sciences
profile.photoAlt: Professional portrait of Lisset Dayana Gómez Pérez
profile.photoText: Top Radiochemistry graduate, scientific merit award recipient, focused on molecular research, teaching and nuclear sciences.
profile.summary: Radiochemistry and Computational Chemistry researcher with experience in molecular modeling, docking, QSAR/QSPR, molecular dynamics, bioactivity, nuclear techniques and university teaching. She connects scientific rigor, computational analysis and clear communication for medicinal chemistry, materials and nanoscopic-process research.

molecular.title: Molecular modeling
molecular.description: Visualization of protein-ligand complexes, catalytic sites, metal coordination, hydrophobic fields and interaction energy.

bioactivity.title: Biological activity maps
bioactivity.description: Integrated map of bioactivity, FRET, cell migration, pharmacophores, angiogenesis networks and QSAR analysis applied to MMP14.

professionalProfile: BSc in Radiochemistry with a strong foundation in physics, general chemistry, mathematical analysis, electrodynamics, optics, thermodynamics, statistics, analytical chemistry, applied radiochemistry, nuclear techniques, quantum chemistry, molecular spectroscopy, radiobiology, radiation safety, radiopharmacology, labeled-compound synthesis, radiotracer metabolism, molecular imaging and computational chemistry. Her profile connects nuclear sciences, molecular modeling, bioactivity and university teaching.

researchProjects.title: Modeling of nanoscopic structures and processes for materials and bioactivity
researchProjects.description: Project from the Faculty of Chemistry at the University of Havana focused on classical, quantum and big-data methods to study materials, bioactivity and nanoscopic systems.
results: Analysis of nanoscopic processes in materials and biological systems

researchProjects.title: Computational chemistry applied to molecular bioactivity
researchProjects.description: Use of molecular modeling, docking and computational descriptors to interpret ligand-receptor interactions, biological activity and pharmacokinetic properties.
results: Computational support for medicinal chemistry research

details: Academic guidance and clear scientific communication.
Teaching foundation connected to radiochemistry and computational chemistry.
Modeling of nanoscopic structures and processes.
Research focused on materials and bioactivity.

Scientific merit award for research results.

Complementary formation for critical thinking and communication.

publications: High standards, critical thinking and self-criticism.
Interests in dance, theater, music and art criticism.
events: International Congress of Chemistry, Biochemistry and Chemical Engineering QUIMICUBA 2024

contact.availability: Available for research, teaching, computational chemistry, radiochemistry, laboratories, academic management, professional service and interdisciplinary scientific projects.
